name: JAK-STAT Signaling Pathway
summary: The JAK-STAT pathway transduces signals from cytokines (e.g., IL-6, interferons, IL-23) and growth factors to the nucleus, regulating immune responses, cell proliferation, and hematopoiesis. JAKs phosphorylate STATs; STAT dimers translocate and drive transcription. Dysregulation contributes to autoimmune disease (e.g., psoriasis, IBD) and cancer. Environmental triggers include infections and inflammatory stimuli. Key genes include JAK2, STAT3, and cytokine receptors; therapeutics target JAK-STAT in psoriasis and rheumatoid arthritis.

environmental_triggers.trigger_type: Chronic stress can modulate inflammatory cytokines
Dietary components may affect systemic inflammation

references.title: Targeting the JAK/STAT pathway in autoimmune disease
